intro: Crantzia schlimii, also known as the "Yellow Passion Flower," is a perennial climber native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the Passifloraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and as a diuretic to alleviate urinary tract issues. The bioactive constituents of Crantzia schlimii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which have been found to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly prepared from the plant's leaves, stems, and roots.
